relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jacob gathered his sons to speak what would meet them ahead.
The sons were called to hear Israel their father.
Reuben was named as first strength and firstborn dignity.
But instability cost him preeminence.
Simeon and Levi were bound together by violent instruments.
Jacob refused to join his soul to their angry council.
Their fierce anger would be divided and scattered.
Judah would be praised, and brothers would bow.
Judah became lion strength.
Rulership was spoken over Judah.
Vine, wine, and abundance surrounded his image.
Wine and milk marked overflowing richness.
Zebulun was set by sea and ships.
Issachar rested like a strong burden-bearing donkey.
Pleasant land became labor-bearing service.
Dan would judge as a tribe of Israel.
Dan would strike like a serpent on the path.
In the middle of the sayings, Jacob waits for the Eternal’s salvation.
Gad would be raided and raid back.
Asher would give rich bread and royal delicacies.
Naphtali would move free and speak beauty.
Joseph was fruitful by the spring.
He was attacked and harassed.
Yet his strength held through the Mighty One, Shepherd, and Stone of Israel.
Blessings came from heavens, deep, breast, and womb.
Blessing crowned Joseph, the one set apart from his brothers.
Benjamin was wolf-force, dividing spoil.
The twelve tribes received words according to their blessing.
Jacob commanded burial with his fathers.
Machpelah was named as the ancestral burial place.
Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, and Leah were remembered there.
The purchased field and cave anchored the family memory.
Jacob finished speaking, gathered himself, and was gathered to his people.
